name: fda-consultant-specialist
description: Senior FDA consultant and specialist for medical device companies including HIPAA compliance and requirement management. Provides FDA pathway expertise, QSR compliance, cybersecurity guidance, and regulatory submission support. Use for FDA submission planning, QSR compliance assessments, HIPAA evaluations, and FDA regulatory strategy development.
capabilities: Analyze FDA regulatory pathways including 510(k), PMA, and De Novo classifications
Guide Quality System Regulation (QSR) 21 CFR 820 compliance implementation
Provide HIPAA compliance framework for medical devices handling PHI
Support FDA submission preparation and management processes
Assist with FDA inspection readiness and warning letter response
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based regulatory consulting skill containing only documentation and guidance. No file system access, network operations, or code execution capabilities. Supporting files are benign placeholders with no security implications.
valueStatement: Medical device companies face complex FDA compliance requirements that delay market entry. This skill provides expert guidance on FDA pathways, QSR requirements, HIPAA compliance, and regulatory submission strategies.
userTitle: Navigate FDA regulations for medical devices
limitations: Cannot provide legal advice or act as a licensed attorney
Cannot file submissions directly to the FDA on your behalf
Does not replace formal regulatory affairs professionals for critical submissions
Guidance is based on published FDA guidance documents and may require verification

useCases.title: Plan regulatory pathway
useCases.description: Determine FDA submission requirements and optimal pathway for novel medical devices

useCases.title: Assess QSR compliance
useCases.description: Evaluate quality system requirements and prepare for FDA inspections

useCases.title: Implement HIPAA safeguards
useCases.description: Develop compliance programs for devices handling protected health information

promptTemplates.title: Device classification
promptTemplates.prompt: My medical device [describe device] is being developed for [intended use]. Help me determine the FDA regulatory pathway and classification requirements.

promptTemplates.title: QSR compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: We need to implement 21 CFR 820 design controls for our medical device. Walk me through the requirements for design input, output, verification, and validation.

promptTemplates.title: HIPAA assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Our medical device collects patient data. What HIPAA technical and administrative safeguards do we need to implement?

promptTemplates.title: Submission strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare a comprehensive 510(k) submission checklist for our device, including required documentation, testing requirements, and timeline considerations.

bestPractices: Engage with FDA early through pre-submission meetings for novel devices
Maintain comprehensive design history files throughout development
Document all regulatory decisions and rationale for audit trails
antiPatterns: Assuming a predicate device is substantially equivalent without detailed comparison
Delaying compliance activities until late in development
Relying solely on this skill for critical regulatory decisions without professional oversight

faq.question: What FDA submission pathways are available?
faq.answer: Three main pathways: 510(k) for substantial equivalence to predicates, PMA for high-risk devices requiring clinical evidence, and De Novo for novel low-to-moderate risk devices.

faq.question: How long does FDA clearance take?
faq.answer: 510(k) averages 6 months but can take longer. PMA takes 12-18+ months. Timeline depends on device complexity, FDA workload, and response to questions.

faq.question: Can this skill file FDA submissions?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance only. You must file submissions through the FDA eCopy system or use a regulatory affairs professional.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. This skill offers regulatory information and guidance but does not constitute legal advice. Consult qualified regulatory counsel for specific situations.

faq.question: What is required for HIPAA compliance?
faq.answer: HIPAA requires administrative safeguards (policies, training), physical safeguards (facility access, device controls), and technical safeguards (encryption, access controls, audit logs).

faq.question: How does this compare to hiring a regulatory consultant?
faq.answer: This skill provides immediate regulatory guidance and information. Human consultants offer personalized strategy, represent you to FDA, and take legal responsibility for submissions.
